perturbation.original_text: Company shall comply with good business practices and all applicable laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall comply with good business practices.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'all applicable laws and regulations' creates a legal contradiction. It removes a general obligation for the company to adhere to laws, potentially harming the reseller if the company violates relevant statutes.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Obligation to follow state and federal regulations regarding business operations
perturbation.law_explanation: This section of the U.S. Code prohibits unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce. By removing the obligation to comply with all applicable laws, the contract now has the potential to allow the company to engage in activities that would violate 15 U.S. Code § 45, harming the reseller.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Declaration of unlawfulness; power to prohibit unfair practices; inapplicability to foreign trade (1) Unfair methods of competition in or affecting commerce , and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce, are hereby declared unlawful. (2) The Commission is hereby empowered and directed to prevent persons, partnerships, or corporations , except banks, savings and loan institutions described in section 57a(f)(3) of this title , Federal credit unions described in section 57a(f)(4) of this title , common carriers subject to the Acts to regulate commerce, air carriers and foreign air carriers subject to part A of subtitle VII of title 49, and persons, partnerships, or corporations insofar as they are subject to the Packers and Stockyards Act, 1921 , as amended [ 7 U.S.C. 181 et seq.], except as provided in section 406(b) of said Act [ 7 U.S.C. 227(b) ], from using unfair methods of competition in or affecting commerce and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce. (3) This subsection shall not apply to unfair methods of competition involving commerce with foreign nations (other than import commerce ) unless— (A) such methods of competition have a direct, substantial, and reasonably foreseeable effect— (i) on commerce which is not commerce with foreign nations, or on import commerce with foreign nations; or (ii) on export commerce with foreign nations, of a person engaged in such commerce in the United States; and (B) such effect gives rise to a claim under the provisions of this subsection, other than this paragraph. If this subsection applies to such methods of compet
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'all applicable laws and regulations,' directly contradicting the original text's explicit requirement for the company to comply with all laws. This creates a potential legal loophole. According to 15 U.S. Code § 45, 'Unfair methods of competition in or affecting commerce, and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce, are hereby declared unlawful.' By removing the obligation to comply with all applicable laws, the company could potentially engage in unfair methods of competition, violating 15 U.S.C. § 45, and harming the reseller. The omission removes a legally mandated protection.

perturbation.original_text: RESELLER SHALL MAKE NO REPRESENTATION, GUARANTEE, OR WARRANTY CONCERNING THE COMPANY PRODUCT EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY AUTHORIZED IN ADVANCE BY COMPANY IN WRITING.
perturbation.changed_text: RESELLER SHALL MAKE NO GUARANTEE, OR WARRANTY CONCERNING THE COMPANY PRODUCT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'REPRESENTATION', the reseller is prohibited from making any claims on the product's behalf, even if true or required by law. This creates a situation where the reseller cannot fulfill obligations related to honest advertising and consumer protection, violating the Virginia Consumer Protection Act.
perturbation.law_explanation: The VCPA prohibits suppliers from misrepresenting the characteristics or benefits of goods or services. By removing the allowance for the reseller to even make representations, the contract implicitly restricts the reseller from accurately representing the product, even to avoid misrepresentation, putting the reseller at risk of violating the VCPA.

perturbation.justification: The original contract allowed the reseller to make representations about the product if authorized by the company. The changed contract removes this allowance, potentially preventing the reseller from accurately describing the product to comply with the Virginia Consumer Protection Act (VCPA), as explained in both the Perturbation and Law Explanations. The law snippet identifies the existence of the Virginia Consumer Protection Act, and the removal of the 'REPRESENTATION' clause directly limits the reseller's ability to make truthful statements, even if needed to avoid misrepresentation under VCPA.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia without regard to the conflicts of law provisions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without regard to the conflicts of law provisions thereof', there can be a situation where another state's or federal law would take precedence over Virginia law and potentially protect the consumer. Removing this phrase reduces protection in the contract for all parties, including the end consumer.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law regarding choice of law
perturbation.law_explanation: The conflicts of law provision allows for different laws to be applied in different situations based on where the infraction occurred or where the parties are from. Removing this allows for only one jurisdiction to be used regardless of where the infraction occurred or if there are federal protections in place.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement is governed by Virginia law without regard to conflicts of law provisions. The changed text removes the phrase 'without regard to the conflicts of law provisions thereof,' which could allow another jurisdiction's laws to take precedence. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'conflict of laws' as a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions connected to a case, where the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law is used. By removing the phrase, the contract now opens itself to conflicts of law that could negatively affect the consumer. This omission creates a legal loophole, as it removes a legally mandated consumer protection, leading to non-compliance.
